title: China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit
summary: China's CAS Space successfully launched its Kinetica-2 rocket for the first time, deploying a prototype cargo spacecraft into orbit. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the Chinese commercial space sector.

title: Pentagon weighing termination of Raytheon GPS ground control contract after years of delays
summary: The Pentagon is considering terminating Raytheon's GPS ground control contract due to years of delays. Space Force is exploring alternatives, including shifting to an upgraded legacy ground system.

title: The race to build orbital data centers is missing its biggest variable: power
summary: The article highlights the growing interest in orbital data centers for AI and computing, but notes a critical gap: the lack of discussion around power sources for these facilities. Major players like Elon Musk, LoneStar, and NVIDIA are driving the narrative, yet energy solutions remain unaddressed.

title: With Voyager’s help, Icarus Robotics to test free-flyer on ISS
summary: Voyager Technologies will assist Icarus Robotics in testing a free-flying platform, Joyride, on the International Space Station. The agreement includes payload integration, safety certification, and launch coordination for a 2027 mission.

title: Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million, achieving a $1.1 billion valuation, to develop orbital data centers. The company is awaiting approval to deploy a network of 88,000 spacecraft.

title: Science finds a place on Artemis 2
summary: Artemis 2 mission astronauts will conduct science experiments during their nearly 10-day test flight. This marks a shift toward integrating research into lunar flyby missions.

title: Satellite imaging industry’s next challenge: getting systems to talk to each other
summary: The satellite imaging industry faces challenges in achieving interoperability between systems due to a lack of incentives and technical standards. Executives highlight the need for 'tipping and cueing' across vendors to improve efficiency.

title: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
summary: NASA is increasing the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions but has not detailed plans to speed up crewed lunar lander development. The agency emphasized a strategy of 'shots on goal' to enhance lunar exploration efforts.

title: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
summary: Starfish Space has secured a new partner for its docking demonstration mission after its original collaborator withdrew. The company is moving forward with revised plans following the change in partnership.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
summary: The four Artemis 2 astronauts have arrived in Florida for final preparations ahead of a planned launch in April. The mission is still scheduled for as soon as April 1.

title: Sun rises on Artemis 2 launch pad | Space photo of the day for March 30, 2026
summary: NASA's SLS moon rocket for the Artemis 2 mission is captured in a striking silhouette against the rising sun at the launch pad. The mission is currently scheduled for April 1, 2026.

title: 'The sasquatch is honesty': Inside Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen's Artemis 2 mission patch
summary: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen's Artemis 2 mission patch incorporates Indigenous perspectives. The design reflects his personal connection to Indigenous heritage.

title: Powerful X-class solar flare triggers radio blackout ahead of Artemis 2 launch (video)
summary: An X1.4 solar flare caused radio blackouts and a fast coronal mass ejection (CME) ahead of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission preparations. The event highlights potential space weather challenges for the upcoming lunar mission.

title: Could bad space weather endanger the Artemis 2 moon astronauts?
summary: Space weather experts are intensifying forecasts ahead of the Artemis 2 mission to mitigate risks for the four astronauts flying around the moon. Concerns exist that solar activity could endanger the crew during their lunar journey.

title: How will Artemis 2 be different from NASA's Apollo moon missions?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission is often compared to the Apollo moon missions, but it differs significantly in its goals and technology. Unlike Apollo, Artemis 2 aims to establish a sustainable human presence on the moon and prepare for future Mars missions.

title: Finished Starfleet Academy? Explore the animated antics of Star Trek: Lower Decks for just $2.99 a month
summary: Paramount+ is offering a $2.99/month deal for two months to watch Star Trek: Lower Decks, an animated series set in the Star Trek universe. This promotion targets fans of the franchise after completing 'Starfleet Academy.'

title: Watch SpaceX launch 119 payloads to orbit from California early on March 30
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 119 payloads to orbit from California early on March 30, with the event available to watch live.

title: 'We are ready': NASA still on track to launch Artemis 2 astronauts to the moon April 1
summary: NASA remains confident in launching the Artemis 2 crewed moon mission on April 1. The agency states it is fully prepared for the historic event.

title: 'Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4': Release date, plot, factions, & everything else we know about the upcoming 40K strategy game
summary: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4 has been announced as the next entry in the real-time strategy series set in the Warhammer 40K universe. Details about the release date, plot, and factions have been teased but not fully revealed.

title: Kristen Stewart will play Sally Ride, the 1st American woman in space, as Amazon finally greenlights mini-series 'The Challenger'
summary: Kristen Stewart will star as Sally Ride, the first American woman in space, in Amazon's newly greenlit mini-series 'The Challenger'. This marks Stewart's debut in television.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon. His career has focused on spacecraft communication, inspired by the Space Shuttle Program.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA awarded Intuitive Machines a $180.4 million contract to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission will carry seven payloads, including five from NASA, to advance lunar research.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate clouds and reveal the surface below.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface imagery of the Pacific Northwest region.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The payloads aim to test thermal protection systems, improve in-space communications, and enhance understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has chosen 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including deploying instruments and collecting Moon rocks. These scientists will help shape the science objectives for astronauts during future lunar missions.

title: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
summary: Michael Guzman, the main propulsion systems engineer for NASA's Artemis II mission, shares his passion for rocket science through his work email signature, which includes complex equations related to rocket thrust and specific impulse.

title: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay
summary: NASA plans to release a BAA under NextSTEP-3, Appendix E, for Project NEXUS to acquire an end-to-end Ka-band relay service. This initiative aims to replace the aging TDRS system with a backward-compatible relay solution.

title: Artemis II Crew’s Suits
summary: NASA and CSA have revealed the Orion Crew Survival System suits for the Artemis II crew, including Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen. The suits will be worn during the upcoming Artemis II test flight.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory America is highlighted for its role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The company's involvement underscores its contribution to deep space exploration efforts.

title: SpaceX to deliver 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit as part of the Transporter 16 mission. This mission will take off from California.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch as part of its commercial spaceflight efforts.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. The mission was revealed by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on the construction and upgrades of Starbase launch pads in preparation for the next-generation Block 3 Starships. This effort highlights SpaceX's focus on increasing launch frequency and operational efficiency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including the U.S., Russia, China, New Zealand, and Norway, are hosting upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired Site 31/6. The mission follows repairs to the launch pad after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. The company is expanding its manufacturing capabilities to support these ambitions.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the upcoming Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the historic lunar mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful reconnection.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber, with launch planned for January 2027 on an Ariane 6 rocket. The spacecraft's 26 ultrasensitive cameras are being exposed to extreme temperatures and vacuum conditions to validate functionality before liftoff.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will launch ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use the new costing software in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communications.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research in space technology and microgravity conditions.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: The Transporter-16 mission successfully launched 119 payloads into orbit. This marks the latest in a series of rideshare missions facilitating multiple payload deployments.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million in a Series A funding round, reaching a valuation of $1.1 billion. The investment underscores strong investor interest in orbital data center ventures and Starcloud's established flight heritage.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million to advance its in-space propulsion technology. The company plans to scale production after flight-qualifying its core technologies.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS network in Low Earth Orbit (LEO). The company aims to provide positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) signals that are stronger, more accurate, and more secure than traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, marked 100 days in office by emphasizing an ambitious goal of returning to the Moon. The announcement signals a renewed focus on lunar missions under his leadership.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them without a clear role. Europe now faces uncertainty regarding its contributions to the Artemis program.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine. This funding marks a significant milestone for the company's advancement in space propulsion technology.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's changing vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has sparked confusion and debate among legislators.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: Directed energy is presented as an alternative to traditional space-based interceptors (SBIs) in a recent article. The post discusses its potential advantages and applications in space defense.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions to streamline regulatory authority. This plan aims to address gaps in existing frameworks for missions that don't fit traditional categories.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating an end-to-end ground system designed for rapid deployment. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations through innovative infrastructure solutions.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The discussion highlights advancements in this technology from AvL's booth.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market outlook due to volatility. Discussions focused on direct-to-direct (D2D) demand, sovereign investments, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discusses the company's RF solutions for LEO satellites at SATShow Week. The interview highlights Qorvo's phased array technologies and their applications in satellite communications.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are increasingly prioritizing sovereign satellite communications networks to enhance data security amid geopolitical tensions. The challenge lies in defining the scope and capabilities of sovereign space infrastructure in a complex geopolitical landscape.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Investors at a SATShow panel highlighted the need for better financing routes to support the growing commercial space sector, which has outpaced its current funding mechanisms.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking space-collected data to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now expanding their networks to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are highlighted as key trends shaping the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators is expected to drive growth.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems and live multi-orbit demonstrations at SATShow Week.
